reviews.review_description:
This game is brutal, doesn’t hold your hand one bit and starts extremely slow. That said, if you stick with it and immerse yourself in the world, you will get things going and the game opens up in a great way. On sale, at full price it’san easy buy

I was absolutely blown away at the amount of content and how the game is even built. A lot of good sized zones with different dungeons and quests all over the world. It truly felt like world of Warcraft or even like RuneScape. Unique mechanics that are awesome too like making your own spell from scratch works really cool!

reviews.review_title: A lot of issues and bugs.
reviews.review_description: It would take me a long time to list everything I encountered in just playing for maybe 6-8 hours max, but I will keep this brief. I don’t recommend this game at all, especially since you can randomly crash and lose all your progress if you haven’t manually saved. (Open and close Meta menu) I didn’t know that at first so I lost hours of progress on that alone, then I lost my T4 Backpack just cause I couldn’t even pick it up after accidentally dropping it. This happens often to, as I put down my T3 sword and couldn’t pick it up. Also died a few times by nothing?  I also think the % are false cause I managed to fail upgrading T3 staff at 90% 4 times in a row. That and many other issues is why I cannot recommend this game. Maybe if a lot of it gets fixed…..

This game offers hundreds, if not thousands,  of hours of gameplay.  It is primarily a PVE game until you reach max level and have all your spells and gear figured out.  Then you can turn the game into a PVP game.  The PVE in this game is challenging until you obtain the gear, knowledge,  and a working strategy - then you can solo anything, although it's still a challenge. 

To be good at pvp requires you to be completely connected to your character,  your strategy,  having the right spells and stat your character intelligently.  The strategy around pvp is deep enough that no one can truly master it, allowing different viable strategies to be introduced that have not been seen before.

The community is diverse.  Most all are friendly.   But this game has no developer activity and you can't see a person's name - allowing full anonymity.   This means anything goes.  Sometimes there will be kids who attack you in the public server.   Just know that it's ok.   You don't really lose anything important (usually).

If you play this (and you should) - ask the community how to create and restore back ups of your character so you can ensure you don't lose your stuff.   Nearly everyone does it,  if you don't,  you're at a disadvantage.

reviews.review_title: Best VRMMO ever - no PvP abuse found
reviews.review_description:
All the old reviews about PvP abuse and beginners being killed while questing are no longer accurate. That’s actually the reason I delayed trying this game  and I regret waiting.

I’ve been playing MMOs for over twenty years, and Oathbreakers VR is the first VR MMO that truly hooked me. It’s a real sandbox with very little hand-holding, and that depth is exactly what makes it special. The world is vast, complex, and rewarding to figure out on your own. I got completely absorbed and played morning till night for days over the holidays without losing interest.

Despite what early reviews suggest, my experience with the community has been the opposite of toxic. Players have been friendly, helpful, and generous with advice. I’ve had no issues progressing as a newer player.

You can solo content, explore endlessly, quest, or team up for dungeons and social play — all at your own pace.

If you enjoy depth, discovery, and an MMO that doesn’t treat you like a child, don’t let the outdated PvP reviews scare you off. This game deserves a healthy population.

if you buy this get a refund the people that play this game are no lives that just sit there and kill you over and over again or if you actually do something they'll kill you once you've gotten something cool or something like that just cuz i think it's funny do not play this game it is dead for a reason

I thought I would try this game. I watched some videos and had it recommended by friends. It is a game that you can lose track of time in. The other players I met are helpful and kind. The quests are fun, I gave it four stars as its early access and I really hope it gets much better in the future. The only thing I found that is a bit of pain is having to hold a button to talk, this seems to be a common gripe when talking to other players. I also wish there was an in game map, even it only shows landmarks and your location. If this not an option a simple compass heading would be very useful.

I uninstalled the game as I have now lost my bag and all its contents several times and had to start over. Getting weapons and upgrading them get money that I lost and buy a basic sword then upgrade that. This all could be solved if all your equipment followed you back to the house when you die. I can understand losing all the cash that you have not put into the bank but as things stand I won't be back.

Too many stats, so many that can overwrite one another. Starting area doesn't have much equipment. And its just too easy to get lost. Plus the talk button makes it hard to roam and talk to others so that you can even get the information needed to play the game.

This is one of the best games I’ve ever played. I always have fun in this game with my friends and this is my go to game every time I hop online however there are some people that are not as friendly as the rest and that will kill you if that feel like it but in my opinion that should be more motivation to get stronger and level up

This game would be good if they didn't allow leveled up players to attack people who don't even have weapons in the entry level. Even runescape had areas where you'd be safe. It's ridiculously stupid that some necromancer looking incel can blast me with spells while I'm in the middle of reading quest info. I understand player vs. player competitiveness, but not when it's so clearly unbalanced, you can't level up if you can't even do anything without dying.  Developers dropped the ball on that big time. Honestly, if I could just have a refund, that would be great. The 30 minute free trial wasn't enough to prepare you for this BS.
The game seems fantastic... But the colour is too dark. I cannot see the floor or the stuff like a berrie !! please put a graphic option to control that ! fix it !

The best game I’ve ever played in my life is one I’ve been playing for about five years now and all I can say is: WOW.

Where do I even start? It has tons of quests, amazing combat, exciting dungeons, and a one-of-a-kind magic system with over 100 spells that are highly customizable to your preferences. You can even choose how you want to cast each spell.

One final thing I’d like to mention is the PvP aspect. There are some toxic PKers (player killers) who may try to attack you, but don’t lose hope there are also plenty of friendly players willing to help. So keep pushing forward, stay strong, and most importantly, have fun!

They don’t sugar coat that this game is difficult. It is. They don’t hold your hand, you can read books to help you, and they can be quite helpful, though the beginning area is made to be more of a safe space. I played on a private server for the first few levels to reduce issues with pvp problems, get the lay of the land, understand the mechanics. Which I feel like was a good idea. But it also meant that I lost out on the ability to do it as a full group. 

The game has one issue that I hadn’t actually ever observed before, and that is, it makes me motion sick, to the point of having to stop and get medication to play. A feeling I haven’t felt in ANY VR game yet. The snap turn setting helps, but not fully. 

The game looks and feels  Morrowind meets alpha EverQuest. It’s got less storyline than Morrowind. Fun, but there also hasn’t been an update since 2023. So it may be abandonware. I have played it for now about 10 hours, and I am enjoying it, after taking Dramamine.

I have been playing for just about a year now and it is probably the best game i have. You have quests to do dungeons to beat, you can even pvp. Although there may be people that kill you its not to big a deal. You can always wait for them to get off of the game or they get bored. Also there are many people that are deicated to helping new players Including myself. A small complant that i have seen is that the early game is very Confusing. I do agree you can always read the beginners guide for help. 

Then you have your stats your build. You can do anything you want. Mage, melee, or archery no matter witch you choose you can make a build for it.

pvp is one of the core mechanics of oathbreakers this is always enabled so there is no possible way to not get attacked by someone. But there are ways to prevent it from happening. First you can stau away from them, second you can wait until there offline, or you can just not (in the persons eyes) be annoying. If someone is being annoying some peoples main instinct is to kill them, preventing them for being annoying. So have fun and explore.

side note. This game does not to many players. But you can fix that, simply try out the game. See if you like, and if you do get it helps. As always have fun, and keep playing.

First half of the game you will be reading books to understand how to play this crap!

You will indeed waste 2 hours figuring out the game sucks but, by then it will be too late. Dont buy I promise and you will not regret it..

One of the best games I've played on VR. Most people are friendly and helpful. There is usually some sort of conversation going on in haven. Now there are a few player killers. But the exploration, dungeons, and quests make the game very enjoyable.  The melee system takes a little getting use to along with spells but it let's you customize your character and play however you like. Hope to see you out there!
IF PEOPLE ARE KILLING YOU JUST GO TO A PRIVATE OR WAIT TILL THEY ARE OFFLINE!!! As a player killer myself I personally tell people to go to a private lobby.

I’ve been on the quest headset for a few years now and I haven’t written a review for any game yet but actually felt inspired to write this. You know what I’ve always wanted? An MMORPG that reminds me of Oldschool RuneScape, WoW or Muu online, but in VR. 

Something mideval/fantasy but not too techy or complicated, and so far, they nailed this one on the head. The game is whimsical, and very much reminiscent of the Legend of Zelda on n64/gamecube. 

The UI/controls are so simple but they also make you figure it out, which so far has created friends and what could end up being a whole community. Most of the peoples I’ve met are very helpful. 

 I was hoping for this in Zenith but that world is pretty dead. VR was MADE for games like this, where you can connect with.  real people in a fantasy world

I was kinda frustrated  when I got killed randomly once but you can tell the developers put a lot of thought into this by making “havens” and then non safe havens.  Your eyes change color when you have bad or good karma. Kind of clever setup. 

The UI and the controls are clean and not complicated. The quests and karma system is unique. I’m stoked to keep playing and building my character. 

Excited to see the development of VR through this type of gameplayand how the game will develop.  This is exactly what VR has needed.

Okay, so this game hooked me right away. I played the demo and then immediately purchased it when the demo ended.

I really like that the game does not hold your hand and makes you figure things out on your own. And there were nice enough people that gave me some hints and tips on how to do certain things.

The game runs pretty smoothly, and I didn't experience very much jank.

Having said that, there are some things that absolutely need to be fixed for a better experience. 

1st and absolutely most important....make the talk button a toggle!!!!!!!. The developer decided that the left controller menu button should be the talk button, and you have to hold it down to talk. This makes walking and talking at the same time almost impossible! And it is one of the harder buttons to push, let alone hold down. 

Second, please, please don't make me wait for a vibration to put things in my inventory slot. So many times, I kept dropping stuff and leaving it behind because I was trying to drop things in my inventory like EVERY OTHER VR GAME KNOWN TO MAN!!!! I don't mind having to wait for vibration when pulling stuff out, but I should just be able to drop things into the slot without waiting. 

Third, why do I have to go to a specific place to find out how much of each item I have, like wood, metal, etcetera? I mean, think about it. I HAVE it why do I have to find someone to tell me how much of something I have on my person. Super annoying. You have some info on your wrist that shows how much sp, and other things I have, why not those as well? 

Fourth, the bag needs to tell you what you have in it. Just have a little menue pop up when you are holding it that says what is in each slot. I don't mind having to take everything out to get to the bottom item, but I want to know what that item is BEFORE I start digging for it. 

And lastly, the sound needs some improvement. Please give me a slight stepping sound and some faint background music or something. sound adds so much to the quality of a game. 

This can easily be a 5 star game. Fix the talking and inventory info, and I will add 2 more stars. 

The game is worth what I spent. I am happy to own and play it. But please MAKE THIS GAME GREAT

the other reviews say it’s a hard game with hackers there is no hackers other than imbue exploits and the game needs to be hard if you need help just ask you can even get basic magic before fighting anyone and if you complain about it look up a guide and explore also you can ask people for help and 9/10 times they will
Amazing game. Even better now that communities have developed.

reviews.review_title: Updated: What VR was made for
reviews.review_description:
My previous review; I’ve been playing this for a few months, it’s the type of game I’d hoped for when I got my headset!

I will leave the 5 stars for posterity, but sadly I would no longer recommend this game, it had the potential to be brilliant, unfortunately the dev seems to have given up, there have been no updates or fixes since 2023.  The game now is rife with hackers and modders who will try to get you to subscribe to their cheats.  What was once a friendly (generally) community has become somewhat toxic.

If you’re interested in giving it a go, I would highly advise playing with friends in a private server and staying clear of the public drama.


Hopefully in the future the dev will return, but for now save your money!
Amazing game would recommend for the experience but dev disappeared last update desember 2023 game is dying so sad but good game would recommend 🧔🏿‍♀️

reviews.review_title: Such a good game, NEEDS UPDATE!
reviews.review_description: So much potential in this game. Got hooked on this game and have maxed my account. This game would really benefit from an update and some attention. Not sure what’s happened with the developer but they have a real gold mine on their hands if they take the time to progress this
Love this so far! Love the old-school vibe, the community is amazing 👏 this is now my favorite game in VR. Keep up the great work Devs!

Needs some work regarding gameplay as when imbuing spells it should display the combo for the spells learned great idea for skill system however the glitching of the ground level and boundary sense need to be adjusted so we're not having to change roomscale level to reach items on ground the whole backpack aspect should have more slots and be able to have a button assigned for item in bag selecting than having to dump out to get the item u want and have equitable and crafting Armour and jewelry for stat and Armour perks and being able to enchant bows swords and other items to  make  things a bit easier to survive but overall I'd give it a 3 and 1/2 outa 5 and have the pvp disabled and have a set designated area which can allow us to loot other players for their items and sp not just being able to be killed anywhere  which I understand other players were getting discouraged with this point to have to go private if you put these things in aspect of the game you will save your population instead of loosing players to a dead game

I have put in some serious hours in this game and it truly is the best game I've played on VR. Lots of different stats to put your talent points into, 7 different elements, a respectable "build-your-own-spell" system. The dev has done a remarkable job, and isn't even finished yet! For being early access it is very well polished. Show the dev some love and come join the community!

This is a really well made fun polished mmo for 20 bucks! Totally worth it compared to similar titles that are unfinished or super buggy.

It isn't for everyone, it is a bit challenging but there is a discord and a great player base to help you get started.

During the trial and early game play make your own server id and play private not public!!!! This is an open pvp game and a few people will kill you at the starting zone although 90 percent of people are nice.

Good luck have fun and I will meet you there!
It's the only good vrmmorpg available. All the others are trying their best to do what this one has done.

reviews.review_title: Great game but no developer anymore….
reviews.review_description:
How long will the game last without a developer? 

developer, Are you still there? please respond!

A big, beautiful, deep, and very well-developed game. It's amazing considering it was created by just one person. There are only two minor drawbacks: 1. The combat system is like flat-screen games. In 2024, that's not very exciting anymore. 2. Multiplayer. As strange as it sounds, this MMORPG is much more enjoyable solo, when you wander through this magical world and explore it peacefully. When no one is chatting around you, standing in your way, or trying to help you. Seriously, you should play this game without guides and alone. Only then does it truly shine in all its glory.

I can’t stop playing this game! If you like adventure, crafting, portals, multiple stories and ways to make money. This game is it! It starts off slow but once you start leveling up and earning money it gets crazy! I saw someone say they deleted it cause the open forum. But the beginning room you get dropped in has an option of solo world or one with ppl in it, n there’s a bank. Games awesome waiting for upgrades!

I tried the trial version of the game and although I didn’t figure much out in that time I decided to buy it. My SO and I have started playing it daily now and the sense of discovery is unmatched in VR. When I finally realized how mining and chopping works, how to upgrade gear and get a steady supply of currencies the game seriously opens up. It is tough at first but perseverance goes such a long way in this game. There are so many things to find and do just in the starting area where you can defintely get your moneys worth before you even really start to explore

I have been playing for around a week now and let me tell you this game is phenomenal. No it’s not graphically pleasing and yes it has some bugs however the gameplay elements are what make this game shine! Between 900 different spells go learn and needing to practice sword swings to get higher damage outputs and the social aspect it really fills me with excitement for the future. This game has 33 different bosses currently and many many quests and areas to explore. I highly recommend this game if you loved runespace or semi hardcore RPG’s. It will amaze you

reviews.review_title: Can't get enough of this game
reviews.review_description: Similar to runescape with the crafting and adventures. It's tough and slow at the start not knowing how to do anything but if you can keep trying it becomes more and more fun
This game sucks, nobody in my server. It didn’t even teach me how to do things!? Would rate this 0 if I could honestly.

I love this game and although I haven’t had it for long I have squeezed every living moment of my life trying to complete these quests 😅whenever I’m playing it on school nights I think to myself “this is the last quest I’m going to do and then I’ll do my homework”. Now I have 10 assignments due for next week.

Welcome to oathbreakers, a runescape-esque game that is as unforgiving as it is rewarding. 

Don't be turned off by the lack of tutorial as many people in game are helpful to new players. 

Combat is fun and varied, along with the multitude of dungeons and raids.

As fun as the game is there was a random person who spawned killing low level players he killed me and I lost server points unless they make the main world PvP free I’m not gonna get on 

Wow also just got back on for an hour than some random hit me with a spelled that stopped me than oneshot me with a sword what a 20 dollar waste do not recommend if you don’t like spawn 

Another spawnkiller killed me twice wish I could give this 0 stars

This is a great game amazing graphics and combat and magic system. It needs improvement but the worst of this game is the toxic players. The papers that go after everyone and will not leave them alone. I am all for pvp however there has to be a way so people can enjoy the game without a constant threat of being murdered while wandering.

As a first purchase this game was well worth it with an amazing combat system and has amazing potential to grow. Whilst the graphics may not be the best the gameplay makes up for it. Most people are nice but if new it’s best to play in a private server due to a few toxic players but overall an amazing game!

reviews.review_title: Literally best multilayer rpg on quest
reviews.review_description:
An early alpha online rpg that beats all competitors its literally my best game on meta 3 and it's not even early access yet.

Good grind, heavy crafting heavy player customising private server you can set and join prevents pvp or allows you to group and play in peace.

Checkout YouTube slightlymad79 for footage/mechanics it's awesome

As a longtime player and a (infamously) known player within the community, i can say this VR game is one of my best buy’s of all time. it easily can eat up the hours without feeling like that much time has passed. Most of the time in game is used socializing in the starter area where all new players spawn, and you can find varying skill and experienced players. obviously this game is challenging and VERY hard to pick up(i’ve yet to find a single person who has picked up this game and learned its mechanics within the first explanation). Overall this is an amazing experience to have and it’s unique to each individual person
Now here’s a few pointers:
-be grateful for the battery warning! 
 •there are many players who’ve lost ALL the progress on their character due to the headset dying in the middle of a fight, as this game doesn’t auto save unless you leave zones or manually quick save by opening your oculus menu while not in combat. this was an issue in the early days of players headsets saying a battery percentage and either crashing or dying mid fight and their files become corrupted, reverting their character, or worse, deleting all the saved files. 
-Be nice as a newcomer
 •newbies usually more curious and eager to play with the big kids. That’s normal! but when you refuse help in a rude manner or are persistently pestering one or multiple people because you didn’t get the answer you wanted, it irritates us. As there is no nameplates and the UI in the game is minimal, we’ve learned to remember voices well and play styles. we remember who you are and what you’ve said, so be smart!
-Don’t give up!
 •lastly and most importantly, do not give up! this game will be difficult and you will die many many times! that’s part of the experience . persistence is key and trust me it’s worth it. with there being so many ways to play this game, you’ll eventually find your own style. Just have fun and stay for the ride. 

25% off if you click this link btw, i hope to see you in there. Tell me you found me from the review and i’ll have a gift for you in game! happy playing :)

oculus . com/appreferrals/lllussion/4757408887694338/?utm_source=oculus&utm_location=4&utm_parent=frl&utm_medium=app_referral

reviews.review_title: i wish they would fix this
reviews.review_description: i played it for a while and it was a great game and i didnt have any problems then i didnt play for about 1 month and i came back and every time i would start the game it would het stuck on the black loading screen. i have tried deleting save data removing it and adding it back to my library and uninstalling and reinstalling it but nothing works. this is literally the only problem i would have with this game. i was trying for so long to figure out a way to fix it and i cant. and its not just me there is another review like this too. if this gets fixed its a instant 5 star but im giving it a 1 star cause i cant even play the game i spent 20$ on
Every time I open the game I get met with a black screen and nothing ever loads please help

reviews.review_title: One Star to make a point.
reviews.review_description:
Tonight I got off work, long day... I wanted to play some Oathbreakers. (Lucky me, I bought it the other day). I plugged my headset up, forgot to last night... oops. It's at 13% still, cool. Time to fire up Oathbreakers...
I see a screen that simply says "Your headset battery is low. Charge your headset before restarting." and that's it... No continue, nothing...
I'm sorry but I paid for this game. It shouldn't matter if my headset is running off the power of a potato and two wires, if I want to open this game I should be able to.
If you want to do a screen that says "Hey, your battery is kinda low... You should really consider charging it before starting." great! Thank you so much for the consideration. But to not allow me to play a game I paid MY money to play because YOU think my battery is low?! You're ridiculous. While it's plugged directly into the wall even. I have never had a game tell me that I can't play because my battery was low. Your game's cool and all but it's not your call at the end of the day if I get to log into the game before bed or not.
Also, your game's not "you get no tutorial" good, humble yourselves, make a little tutorial... The complexity of your mechanics aren't in the mechanics, they're in learning them with nothing showing you how they're intended to work while not being common or intuitive... It doesn't look deep and  intriguing, it looks lazy... And your "people of lower IQ may find this game challenging" pfft... Humble yourself.

The game itself is fun for a little while until you realize it's just a bunch of things copy pasted from the unity asset store. The developer is a single person that at first sounds cool because one guy alone built a game but that fact rears it's head unbelievably quick. The community is the most toxic I've EVER experienced as of recently. If you're a fan of a runescape like vr experience and having people accuse you of sexually assaulting your children with no consequence, then this is the game for you!

I'll be honest, the concept and everything about the game is fantastic, it has all the elements that I want to see in other open world-esque games on the quest, and it is fantastic in that regard, but I have a few issues that really demoralise me as a player.

-I play sitting down because of underlying health conditions, so my hope is that there will eventually be a system in place that allows to crouch or change the hight.

-I knew going in that the game doesn't hold your hand, but  it really doesn't tell you anything! I play games to enjoy myself, but I feel as if I have to take a 3 hour session at a library to even grasp the basic concept. It does just feel as if you've been abandoned by the game, there IS a reason why most to all games have a tutorial to at least introduce you to the basics, or it just turns away new players.

-I feel like I don't get my worth out of a £14 game that drops me into a world with no preexisting knowledge or context, compared with other games on the quest for about the same price, it feels like the game has everything, but has mastered nothing another game can't do better. Whilst it has many features, we've seen them implemented better in other vr titles,it really feels like you just feel like you bought a knock off version of those games.

I feel awful for leaving such a negative review, and the explanation is that it probably just isn't a game for me, but never before have I so dissatisfied with a game that I've ever wanted to return it, but I can't, so use that £14 to implement some degree of a tutorial? Or not, I guess, I'll be honest, I'm kind of just done, I'm done

reviews.review_title: This is not a good game
reviews.review_description:
This game for as long as it has been out does not show quality in many aspects that not just vr games but games in general require. 

The only fun mechanic in the game is magic which is barred initially behind a bunch of quests, and even then you have to reroll so many aspects of them to tune the spell you want. But to get other spells the process is so arduous. 

The dungeons aren’t fun, the npc’s are as basic as it gets as far as them getting up and running to their spawn zone once they’ve been dead enough. 

The combat leaves a lot to be desired on top of that with everything being cool-down based activation items, everything down from swords bows to your hands for magic it just feels so barebones overall. 

The game definitely has potential to grow into a great vrmmo but as it stands it’s not worth the purchase as by the time you’ve figured out with the slow start what you’re doing it may be too late to refund when you find you don’t want to do any of it
I love the pickpocket system, but a lot of people get so emotionally self righteous over a game mechanic. Lots of slurs
Press the 3 lines on your left controller

Love this game! It is the best RPG fantasy game on the Quest 2. There are a lot of areas to explore, dungeons to raid, and quests to be completed. The character development is fluid  and the magic system is very cool. There is an interesting karma system in the game that makes it quickly apparent what type of character you are interacting with. It is possible to combine your favorite RPG play styles going beyond limitations of a simple thief, ranger, paladin, mage, or assassin. This game doesn’t hold your hand in the early stages, but it provides sufficient clues to get you started. The community is helpful and friendly. If you like PVP, there is an arena that provides a great area to test your battle prowess. It is by far my favorite game to play, and it is only anticipated to get better.

Purchased this game two years ago and didn’t get into it, came back recently after they brought it to app lab and I’ve been playing non stop amazing game it doesn’t hold your hand but if you figure things out it pays off

This is exactly what I've been waiting for. Hands down best game I've played on here. I look forward to seeing this game progress and I would definitely recommend it.

It remind me of the old school games that I miss. I love that you gotta go figure stuff out and there's no exclamation points and markers over everything. 

If you liked old school MMORPG you will enjoy this. Definitely worth checking playing.

reviews.review_title: this game, holy sh--t the detail!!!
reviews.review_description: every quest filled with detail,  house entered, and tree chopped this world is always changing  and everything feels smooth this game is going to pop off PLEASE IM BEGGING YOU NEVER GIVE THIS UP THIS GAME WILL POP OFF SOON I BELIVE
Having a great in this game, there is depth unlike other mmorpgs out there.
Womp womp

I'm rating 3 stars because I had my backpack on The ground and some guy saw it and he thought  it would be funny to show his friends his new spell that he's been showing off for 5 hour to kill me. I just lost so much progress I'm really mad about this
Super detailed and in depth game. Super excited for what is in store for it in the future. You can join the community to level up faster and learn more about the game

reviews.review_title: I'm Addicted, this game is GOOD.
reviews.review_description: Dont Get me wrong, I know this game can be frustrating but if you love medieval MMO's this could be up your alley. ive spent the last week thinking about this non-stop. GIVE IT A TRY, ITS WORTH IT.

reviews.review_title: Best rpg game (25% off referral link)
reviews.review_description:
This is the best rpg game ever, spells, dungeons, swords, everything, it’s all about progress in this game and meeting new people. Use this referral link for 25% off once you buy the game. https://www.oculus. com/appreferrals/TheGoatedShortOne/4757408887694338/

delete the space before .com
